title: nepeta kurramensis
intro: Nepeta kurramensis, also known as Kurram mint, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Afghanistan and Pakistan. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Nepeta kurramensis include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory problems such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Nepeta kurramensis can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
